1999 1998 1997 1996 1995 1994 1993 1992 1991 1990 1989 1988 1987 1986 1985 1984 Skip to sidebar Operational Semantics of Hybrid Systems Edward A. Lee and Haiyang Zheng Invited paper in Hybrid Systems: Computation and Control: 8th International Works

: Computation and Control: 8th International Workshop, HSCC , LNCS 3414, Zurich, Switzerland, March 9-11, 2005 Prepublished version Published version ABSTRACT This paper discusses an interpretation of hybrid systems as executable models. A specificat

hybrid systems as executable models. A specification of a hybrid system for this purpose can be viewed as a program in a domain-specific programming language. We describe the semantics of HyVisual, which is such a domain-specific programming languag

hich is such a domain-specific programming language. The semantic properties of such a language affect our ability to understand, execute, and analyze a model. We discuss several semantic issues that come in defining such a programming language, such

come in defining such a programming language, such as the interpretation of discontinuities in continuous-time signals, and the interpretation of discrete-event signals in hybrid systems, and the consequences of numerical ODE solver techniques. We de
equences of numerical ODE solver techniques. We describe the solution in HyVisual by giving its operational semantics. Contact Update Help Update Copyright © 1999 - UC Regents; all rights reserved.
